sections_cited: section 47 of the Sales Tax Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Special STRA 74 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.1, 4, 27 & 30. 2. For orders on CMA No.1521/2024. 3. For orders on CMA No.1522/2024. 4. For orders on CMA No.1523/2024. 5. For hearing of main case. 13.05.2026 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, advocate holding brief for Mr. Munawwar Ali Memon, advocate for the applicant. Matter is pending since 2024 without any progress. On 04.12.2024, following order was passed: “04.12.2024 Mr. Munawar Ali Memon, Advocate for Applicant. 1. Counsel is not prepared to assist in any manner as to seeking the condonation of delay in this case. Adjourned.” Prima facie matter is time barred and applicant has made no endeavor in the last three years to have the matter listed or heard. Even today, brief is being held and adjournment is being sought for no cogent reason. It appears that applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 47 of the Sales Tax Act, 1990. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
